name: exif
title: exif
summary: command-line utility to show EXIF information in JPEG files
description:
command-line utility to show EXIF information in JPEG files
Most digital cameras produce EXIF files, which are JPEG files with
extra tags that contain information about the image.
.
'exif' is a small command-line utility to show EXIF information hidden
in JPEG files.

commands.name: exif
commands.description: shows EXIF information in JPEG files

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use exif because command-line utility to show EXIF information in JPEG files.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use exif as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `exif [ OPTION ] [ file... ]` as documented upstream for exif.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `exif image.jpg` as documented upstream for exif.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `exif --list-tags --no-fixup image.jpg` as documented upstream for exif.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `exif --tag=XResolution --no-fixup image.jpg` as documented upstream for exif.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair exif with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `exif --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: exif - shows EXIF information in JPEG files
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install exif. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `exif [ OPTION ] [ file... ]`. Further invocations from upstream: `exif image.jpg`; `exif --list-tags --no-fixup image.jpg`. command-line utility to show EXIF information in JPEG files.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: command-line utility to show EXIF information in JPEG files.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: exif - shows EXIF information in JPEG files.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install exif
Install with: sudo apt install exif
